metadata.title:
CORPORATIONS


BUY PAPERS
--sr... XII"

COMBINED CoWLES PLANT
REPORTED SOLD AND NEW
MAN Is PLACED IN CHARGE.
G. NJ. OR STANDARD OIL
DEAL.

pages.raw_text:
The report that w. H. Cowles
has disposed of his combined
newspaper plant in this city, in
Guiding the Spokesman-Review and
the Chronicle is accepted as fact
In business circles. Mr.. Cowles,
1t is understood, will 60 abroad for
several years, when the details of
the deal have been completed. w.
d. Finley, who came to Spokane a
year ago with the announced in
tention of starting a new daily
here. backed by the Boston Globe,
is now nominally in charge of the
Cowles combine. and has been
actively concerned with the publ
cation of the Chronicle end of 1t
for the past week or so. 1t is ex-
pected that he will shortly take
entire charge of the plant, repre-
jsenting the purchasers.


! Who the latter are is not d.
vulged by those who profess to be
on the inside, but the common ac-
lceptance is that either the Great
Northern or Rockefeller interests
are concerned. They are to be
represented locally by syndicate
of Spokane businessmen who. to
all public intents and purposes,
will be the actual owners.


After four years or more of
undertaking and general exposure,
the great corporations Of the coun-
try have learned the value of sex-
tensive publicity facilities, and the
deal here is merely one of a num-
Der that have been reported from
various parts of the country and
not generally disputed. The pub
lie appears to need edueation in
order to appreciate the benefits of
corporation domination, and the
corporations are willing to pay for
the education, choosing newspa-
pers as the best mediums, rather
than encounter anything like what
they have had to meet under the
Roosevelt administration.


1t is probable that the local pur-
chase of newspaper property is a
Great Northern deal, contaminated
at the time of Hill and Elliott's
visit week ago. The report of
an impending sale to this company
became current when Mr.. Cowles
went to st. Paul last spring. The
Hill roads will have great deal to
do with public affairs in this part
Of the country in the next few
years. A first page interview with
Hill and Elliott after the visit here
on the mistake made in the Spo-
kane grade separation matter was
the first indication : the Review
gave of a new influence in its
midst.


Statements on the amount of
money involved in the deal are not
available.
